shower.thought: We spend our whole lives learning how to be ourselves, but by the time we figure it out, we're supposed to become someone else.
You've never actually seen your own face—only reflections and photographs of it. You're the only person who doesn't know what you look like.
The saddest thing about getting older isn't losing youth—it's realizing how many conversations you had with people you'll never see again, and you didn't know it at the time.
Every parent is just an adult doing their best impression of someone who knows what they're doing.
You probably have songs in your head that no one will ever hear, and somewhere, someone has the song you're looking for that they'll never share.
The reason you can't remember most of your life is probably a feature, not a bug—imagine being conscious of every boring moment.
If a tree falls and you film it, did the tree make a sound or did you make a video?
People assume you're lying about small things because they're lying about small things. Trust is just statistical guess-work.
Your phone knows you better than anyone who claims to know you well.
Being punctual is just anxiety about being late, and being late is just anxiety about rushing.
The worst part about knowledge is that you can't unfact something. Once you know, you know forever.
You have never actually finished anything—you've only stopped working on it.
We convince ourselves we're too busy to do the things we actually want, but we're never too busy for things we're avoiding.
The person you were last year would be confused by half the things you believe now.
Sleep is the only socially acceptable way to escape reality for eight hours, which is why we're all so good at it.
Every group of friends has one person who's actually just tolerating everyone else, and they might be you.
You can't improve something you're not willing to admit is broken.
The saddest part about getting therapy isn't your problems—it's realizing you paid a stranger to care about you.
Your biggest regrets are probably things you didn't do, but you'll never know about them because they didn't happen.
Your taste in music says less about who you are and more about who you wanted to be at the time.

motivational.thought: The only way out is through. — Robert Frost
Your future self will thank you for what you do right now. — Unknown
Discipline is choosing what you want most over what you want now. — Unknown
Progress whispers while perfection screams. Choose the whisper. — Unknown
You're not behind. You're exactly where you need to be to become who you're meant to be. — Unknown
The most powerful words in existence are "I'll start today." — Unknown
Regret for things not done is far worse than regret for things done wrong. — Unknown
Build the life you want, not the life you think you should want. — Unknown
Every expert was once a beginner who refused to quit. — Unknown
Your potential is not some distant trophy. It's inside every choice you make today. — Unknown
The difference between who you are and who you want to be is what you do. — Bill Phillips
Waiting for inspiration is like waiting for permission you don't need. — Unknown
Small changes seem insignificant until they're not. — Unknown
You cannot afford the luxury of a negative thought. — George Matthew Adams
Do it scared. Do it anyway. Do it until it stops being scary. — Unknown
Your life does not improve by chance. It improves by change. — Unknown
The person you'll become is being decided by the person you are right now. — Unknown
Momentum beats motivation every single time. — Unknown
Excellence is not a skill, it's a habit. — Aristotle
The moment you stop making excuses is the moment your life changes. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought: My wife asked me why I never listen to her. At least I think that's what she said, I wasn't really paying attention.
I'm reading a book on the history of glue. Can't put it down.
My therapist asked if I have any regrets. I said, "Only that I can't afford more therapy."
I told my friend his business was failing. He said, "How do you know?" I said, "You told me last week you were doing great."
My dad said, "Son, I'm proud of you." I said, "Thanks, Dad." He said, "I wasn't talking to you."
I have a stepladder because my real ladder left when I was just a kid.
My girlfriend said I was immature. I told her to get out of my treehouse.
The doctor said I needed to cut back on my drinking. I found a new doctor.
I asked my boss for a raise. He said, "You're doing great work." I said, "So that's a yes?" He said, "No, that was the rejection speech I use so people don't cry."
I went to a motivational speaker once. He said, "You can do anything you set your mind to." I've been setting my mind to napping ever since.
My wife left me because I'm a pessimist. Oh well, I saw that coming.
I told my friend he was being paranoid. He said, "Are you sure you're not just pretending to be my friend?" And honestly, he might be onto something.
I started going to AA meetings. Turns out it's for Alcoholics Anonymous, not Angry Accountants. Completely different vibe.
My doctor told me I have six months to live. I said, "I can't afford the bills." He said, "Okay, twelve months."
I'm not saying my family is dysfunctional, but our family tree is more of a family stump.
My kid asked me what happens after we die. I said, "Nobody knows." He said, "Then why did Grandpa say it was peaceful?" I said, "He was asleep."
I told my psychiatrist I felt like nobody understands me. He said, "Exactly."
My wife said she needed space. I locked her in the garage—problem solved.
I asked my Uber driver if he liked his job. He said, "It's great until I pick up passengers." I said, "Fair enough."
I haven't spoken to my brother in five years. He's still angry about something, but honestly, I've forgotten what. Probably works in my favor.
